patient_profile.name: Alyssa Harris
patient_profile.lifestyle_summary: Alyssa is a 15-year-old high school student living in a suburban neighborhood. She engages in moderate physical activity, has a preference for modern medicine, and maintains a high-salt diet. She has a history of heavy alcohol consumption and formerly smoked but quit over a year ago.
patient_profile.past_medical_history: Diagnosis of childhood asthma, resolved by age 12. No other significant medical history.
patient_profile.family_history: Father has ADHD (diagnosed in adulthood). Mother has a history of anxiety. No notable history of other severe medical or psychiatric conditions in immediate family.
patient_profile.medication_history: Previously prescribed albuterol for asthma (discontinued). No current medications for other health conditions.
patient_profile.allergy_history: No known drug or food allergies.
patient_profile.vaccination_history: Up-to-date on routine childhood and adolescent immunizations, including HPV and Tdap.

symptom_trajectory.chief_complaint: I keep struggling to focus on homework and exams, and I can't sit still during class. It’s been getting worse this year.
symptom_trajectory.onset: Symptoms of inattention and hyperactivity have been present since early childhood but worsened noticeably around age 14 when academic demands increased.
symptom_trajectory.duration: Symptoms have been persistent for over a year, with no periods of remission.
symptom_trajectory.progression: Gradual worsening of both inattentive and hyperactive symptoms, particularly impacting academic performance and social interactions.

selected_symptoms.name: difficulty sustaining attention
selected_symptoms.impact: Frequently unable to complete reading assignments or focus during lengthy lectures.

selected_symptoms.name: poor organization
selected_symptoms.impact: Difficulty keeping track of deadlines and maintaining an orderly homework plan.

selected_symptoms.name: restlessness
selected_symptoms.impact: Frequently shifts in her seat and taps her fingers during class, distracting herself and peers.

selected_symptoms.name: excessive talking
selected_symptoms.impact: Interrupting teachers and classmates during lessons and struggling to wait her turn in conversations.

symptom_trajectory.functional_impact: Moderate impairment in academic performance, reduced ability to participate in structured group activities, and some strain in peer relationships due to interruptive behaviors.
risk_features: No evidence of severe emotional distress, substance abuse, or legal issues.
